page: Microsoft Azure FX48mds_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at a high clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 1008.0 GB of RAM, yielding 21.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 2835 GB, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. The x86_64 architecture includes advanced CPU flags such as AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE and AMX_INT8) for accelerated vector and matrix operations.

Benchmark data indicates strong performance across memory and processing workloads. The server achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 2338.0, placing it in the top tier, and a multi-core score of 20347.0. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 4138.71 GB/sec and large block uncached read bandwidth at 330.82 GB/sec. Database operations are supported by strong Passmark database scores of 28908.89 and Redis SET performance of 74.99 million operations per second. For computational tasks, the system demonstrates strong performance in software compilation and image processing.

Although the server does not feature dedicated GPU hardware, its Intel Xeon processor with AMX flags supports local LLM inference. It processes prompts at 2674.21 tokens/sec for small models and 33.02 tokens/sec for large models, though text generation speeds for larger models remain average at 3.45 tokens/sec. Given its massive memory capacity, high-frequency CPU, and local storage, this instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, and data-heavy enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure FX48mds_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs and 1008.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance includes 2835 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. It delivers strong memory performance, with uncached large-block read bandwidth reaching 330.82 GB/sec and Redis SET operations exceeding 74.99 million operations per second. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel AMX, which contribute to top-tier Geekbench single-core and image processing scores. Lacking a dedicated GPU, the server relies on its high-frequency CPU and massive memory footprint to handle demanding workloads. It is highly suited for memory-intensive databases, large-scale in-memory caching, software compilation, and enterprise data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX48mds_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 1008 GB of RAM, and 2835 GB of local storage, designed for high-performance database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX48mds_v2 is a memory-optimized VM with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 1008 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-intensive virtual machine featuring one terabyte of RAM and fast local storage for demanding database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs operating at a high clock speed of 4.2 GHz.
1008.0 GB of RAM providing 21.0 GB of memory capacity per core.
2835 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated vector and matrix math.
Strong database performance with 74.99 million Redis SET operations per second.
Optimized for memory-intensive databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications.
